title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Please watch the entire video before caring for your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: This video will teach you how to care for your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: An incision is a cut made in your skin during an operation.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: The edges of your incision may be held together with stitches, staples, skin glue, or skin

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: closure strips.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: A bandage called addressing covers your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: To prevent skin infections, keep your dressing clean and dry.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Taking good care of your incision will help it heal more quickly.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Caring for your incision involves cleaning your incision and changing your dressing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: You will need New bandages A roll of medical tape

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Clean scissors Two pairs of disposable gloves

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Two clean, soft cloths or paper towels A disposable plastic bag

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: And a waste basket.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Keep these materials away from your incision to reduce your risk of infection.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Prepare materials

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 1.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Wash your hands with soap and water for 30 seconds and then dry them with a clean towel or paper towels.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Clean hands are the best protection against spreading infection.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 2.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Open a new bandage wrapper.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Leave the bandage inside the wrapper and be careful not to touch it.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 3.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Cut four pieces of tape and hang them from the edge of a nearby table or counter.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: The pieces of tape should be the same length as the old ones around your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Remove old dressing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 1.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Wash your hands again with soap and water and then dry them.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 2.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Put on disposable gloves.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 3.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: With one hand, press down gently on the skin next to the old tape around your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: With the other hand, carefully peel away the old tape toward your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 4.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: After loosening all of the tape, remove your old dressing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Do not remove any skin closure strips covering your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Let them fall off on their own in about 5-10 days.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: If your dressing sticks to your incision, don't tear it off.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Tearing it off may cause bleeding and delay healing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Moisten the dressing with warm water.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Wait for a minute or two until you can easily remove it.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Look at your old dressing after you remove it.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: If the dressing has a foul or sweet smell or it has yellow or greenish stain, call your

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: healthcare provider.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 5.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Place the old dressing in a disposable plastic bag.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 6.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Remove the disposable gloves.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Throw the gloves and the plastic bag containing your old dressing in the waste basket.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Clean the incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Clean your incision only if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Your healthcare provider may only want you to change the dressing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Make sure you understand what your healthcare provider wants you to do and be sure to call

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: with questions or concerns.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 1.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Wash your hands with soap and water and dry them.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 2.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Put on a new pair of disposable gloves.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 3.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Always use a clean cloth, warm water, and if your healthcare provider says you can, a mild

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: antibacterial soap to gently remove dried material from the area around your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Start with the area's nearest urinsision and wipe outwards or away from the incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Use a different part of the cloth each time you wipe your skin.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: When the cloth is dirty, use a new clean cloth.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Do not scrub or remove any scabs.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Scabs protect your incision while it heals.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Allow them to fall off on their own.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Do not wipe or rub over stitches, staples, or skin closure strips.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Unless your healthcare provider says you should, do not clean your incision with alcohol,

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: iodine, or peroxide.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 4.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Let your incision air dry.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: As it dries, check your incision for the following.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Stitches or staples that are out of place.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: The edges of your incision pulling open, bleeding around your incision, redness that extends

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: more than one inch from your incision edges, increased skin warmth around your incision,

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: swelling around your incision, foul or sweet smelling incision odor, or a white, yellow,

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: or green discharge coming from your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Call your healthcare provider if you notice any of these changes.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Unless your healthcare provider says you should, do not use, appointment, lotion, or powder

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: on your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 5.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Remove the disposable gloves and throw them away in the waste basket.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Apply new dressing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 1.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Wash your hands with soap and water and dry them.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 2.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Put on a new pair of disposable gloves.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 3.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Pick up a gauze pad by one corner.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Do not touch the side that will be next to your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Carefully cover your incision with the gauze pad.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: You may need more than one gauze pad if fluid is still coming out of your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 4.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Hold the gauze pad in place with one hand and tape the gauze pad to your skin with the

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: other hand.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Make sure that you seal all four edges of the dressing.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: If your skin is red from the tape on your old dressing, put the new tape on a different

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: part of your skin.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: If your skin is swollen or has blisters, contact your healthcare provider.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: You may be allergic to the tape and need to try a different kind.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 5.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Put your gloves and put them into the waste basket.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Step 6.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Wash your hands again with soap and water and dry them.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: It's normal to see mild redness and swelling around your incision edges or a clear or pinkish

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: discharge.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: However, you should contact your healthcare provider if you notice.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Any white, yellow or green discharge coming from your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: A foul or sweet incision odor, the shape of your incision changes.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Redness that extends more than one inch from your incision edges, increased warmth of the

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: skin around your incision, and bleeding or swelling around your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: Also call your healthcare provider if you have nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or a temperature

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
text_en: of 101 degrees Fahrenheit or higher.
